title: Quiz error and recovery state
prompt: Improve the quiz experience so failed or invalid quiz generation has a visible inline recovery path, preserves any prior completed quiz result, and remains usable in deterministic demo mode.
judgeFocus: evaluator evidence quality for runtime failures

scale.1: Fails the dimension or has no usable evidence.
scale.2: Has major gaps, regressions, or mostly claim-based evidence.
scale.3: Acceptable but incomplete, noisy, or only partially evidenced.
scale.4: Strong result with minor gaps or limited missing evidence.
scale.5: Excellent result with complete behavior and strong evidence.

criticalRequirements: Failed or invalid quiz generation must have a visible inline recovery path.
Prior completed quiz results must remain available after a later generation failure.
Deterministic demo mode must remain usable and predictable.
The implementation must avoid broad unrelated tutor, dashboard, or provider refactors.

dimensions.description: How completely the final workspace improves quiz failure handling, recovery UX, prior-result preservation, and deterministic demo behavior.
Whether tests, typecheck, failure paths, persisted quiz state, and demo/live boundaries behave correctly without introduced regressions.
Maintainability, smallness of the diff, fit with existing quiz state contracts, clear error-state modeling, and focused test coverage.
Quality of research, sprint planning, contract specificity, repair-loop behavior, and whether runtime-failure risks were anticipated.
Whether verdicts are backed by concrete failure/recovery evidence, objective checks, and clear treatment of any unrelated baseline failures.

scoringNotes: Do not change this rubric based on run-generated eval criteria or contracts.
Prefer concrete failure-path evidence over implementation claims.
A run that hides a generation failure instead of providing recovery should score poorly on taskFulfillment and correctness.
